`using Distributed
using Libdl
procs = addprocs(1)
@everywhere procs x =Libdl.dlopen(xx.so)
`
when i use addprocs add a process and open a so in it. the assert in line 54 will fail.
the code in xx.so look like in the picture below:


![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e634da0f-dc4a-4344-9224-edf7686f7ad3)


i use movl to get esp or rsp value，and compare it to stack which get from pthread_attr_getstatck.

in addition：
If I don't use addprocs. like this：
`using Distributed
using Libdl
x=Libdl.dlopen("xx.so")
println(x)
`

the program pass and the assert is not triggered

It literally runs in a different process, so seeing different stack pointers does not seem surprising to me.

yes，different process have different stack pointer.  but when check statck pointer witch stack size in its own process  ,the stack pointer should be in stack range which get from  pthread_attr_getstack","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2403822434/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56064/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56064/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2409815355","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56064#issuecomment-2409815355","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56064","id":2409815355,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6Pot07","user":{"login":"jiudianren","id":9455812,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjk0NTU4MTI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9455812?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jiudianren","html_url":"https://github.com/jiudianren","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jiudianren/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jiudianren/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jiudianren/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jiudianren/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jiudianren/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jiudianren/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jiudianren/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jiudianren/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jiudianren/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-14T02:30:25Z","updated_at":"2024-10-14T02:30:25Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> This probably has got nothing to do with `addprocs`. It's about the stack being checked in a julia task, which runs on its own stack. With the `mylib.so` as above, try
> 
> ```
> julia> @ccall "./mylib.so".checkstack()::Nothing
> ... success=1
> julia> @async @ccall "./mylib.so".checkstack()::Nothing
> ... success=0
> ```
> 
> Because the `@async` creates a `Task`, which gets its own stack. I suppose the same thing is done with remote `Distributed` calls.


I run your code, and  get  your idea. thanks a lot.
